successfulRecords.description: A list of memory records that were successfully created during the batch operation.

failedRecords.description: A list of memory records that failed to be created, including error details for each failure.

memoryRecordId.description: The unique ID associated to the memory record.

status.description: The status of the memory record operation (e.g., SUCCEEDED, FAILED).

requestIdentifier.description: The client-provided identifier that was used to track this record operation.

errorCode.description: The error code returned when the memory record operation fails.

errorMessage.description: A human-readable error message describing why the memory record operation failed.

successfulRecords.description: A list of memory records that were successfully deleted during the batch operation.

failedRecords.description: A list of memory records that failed to be deleted, including error details for each failure.

memoryRecordId.description: The unique ID associated to the memory record.

status.description: The status of the memory record operation (e.g., SUCCEEDED, FAILED).

requestIdentifier.description: The client-provided identifier that was used to track this record operation.

errorCode.description: The error code returned when the memory record operation fails.

errorMessage.description: A human-readable error message describing why the memory record operation failed.

successfulRecords.description: A list of memory records that were successfully updated during the batch operation.

failedRecords.description: A list of memory records that failed to be updated, including error details for each failure.

memoryRecordId.description: The unique ID associated to the memory record.

status.description: The status of the memory record operation (e.g., SUCCEEDED, FAILED).

requestIdentifier.description: The client-provided identifier that was used to track this record operation.

errorCode.description: The error code returned when the memory record operation fails.

errorMessage.description: A human-readable error message describing why the memory record operation failed.

memoryId.description: The identifier of the AgentCore Memory resource containing the event.

actorId.description: The identifier of the actor associated with the event.

sessionId.description: The identifier of the session containing the event.

rootEventId.description: The identifier of the root event for this branch.

eventId.description: The identifier of the event that was deleted.

memoryRecordId.description: The identifier of the memory record that was deleted.

runtimeSessionId.description: The ID of the session associated with the AgentCore Runtime agent.

agentCard.description: An agent card document that contains metadata and capabilities for an AgentCore Runtime agent.

createdAt.description: The time at which the browser session was created.

viewPort.description: The configuration that defines the dimensions of a browser viewport in a browser session. The viewport determines the visible area of web content and affects how web pages are rendered and displayed. Proper viewport configuration ensures that web content is displayed correctly for the agent's browsing tasks.

sessionTimeoutSeconds.description: The timeout period for the browser session in seconds.

status.description: The current status of the browser session. Possible values include ACTIVE, STOPPING, and STOPPED.

streams.description: The streams associated with this browser session. These include the automation stream and live view stream.

sessionReplayArtifact.description: The artifact containing the session replay information.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The time at which the browser session was last updated.

width.description: The width of the viewport in pixels. This value determines the horizontal dimension of the visible area. Valid values range from 800 to 1920 pixels.

height.description: The height of the viewport in pixels. This value determines the vertical dimension of the visible area. Valid values range from 600 to 1080 pixels.

sessionId.description: The identifier of the code interpreter session.

name.description: The name of the code interpreter session.

createdAt.description: The time at which the code interpreter session was created.

sessionTimeoutSeconds.description: The timeout period for the code interpreter session in seconds.

status.description: The current status of the code interpreter session. Possible values include ACTIVE, STOPPING, and STOPPED.

memoryId.description: The identifier of the AgentCore Memory resource containing the event.

actorId.description: The identifier of the actor associated with the event.

sessionId.description: The identifier of the session containing the event.

rootEventId.description: The identifier of the root event for this branch.

memoryRecordId.description: The unique identifier of the memory record.

text.description: The text content of the memory record.

memoryStrategyId.description: The identifier of the memory strategy associated with this record.

namespaces.description: The namespaces associated with this memory record. Namespaces help organize and categorize memory records.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the memory record was created.

apiKey.description: The API key associated with the resource requested.

authorizationUrl.description: The URL to initiate the authorization process, provided when the access token requires user authorization.

sessionUri.description: Unique identifier for the user's authorization session for retrieving OAuth2 tokens. This matches the sessionId from the request and can be used to track the session state.

sessionStatus.description: Status indicating whether the user's authorization session is in progress or has failed. This helps determine the next steps in the OAuth2 authentication flow.

workloadAccessToken.description: An opaque token representing the identity of both the workload and the user.
An opaque token representing the identity of both the workload and the user.
The access token for the specified workload.

mcpProtocolVersion.description: The version of the MCP protocol being used.

traceParent.description: The parent trace information for distributed tracing.

traceState.description: The trace state information for distributed tracing.

contentType.description: The MIME type of the response data. This indicates how to interpret the response data. Common values include application/json for JSON data.

response.description: The response data from the agent runtime. The format of this data depends on the specific agent configuration and the requested accept type. For most agents, this is a JSON object containing the agent's response to the user's request.

statusCode.description: The HTTP status code of the response. A status code of 200 indicates a successful operation. Other status codes indicate various error conditions.

sessionId.description: The identifier of the code interpreter session.

stream.description: The stream containing the results of the code execution. This includes output, errors, and execution status.

result.description: The output produced by executing code in a code interpreter session in Amazon Bedrock. This structure contains the results of code execution, including textual output, structured data, and error information. Agents use these results to generate responses that incorporate computation, data analysis, and visualization.

content.description: The textual content of the execution result. This includes standard output from the code execution, such as print statements, console output, and text representations of results.

structuredContent.description: The structured content of the execution result. This includes additional metadata about the execution, such as execution time, memory usage, and structured representations of output data. The format depends on the specific code interpreter and execution context.

taskId.description: The identifier of the task that produced the result.

taskStatus.description: The status of the task that produced the result.

stdout.description: The standard output from the tool execution.

stderr.description: The standard error output from the tool execution.

exitCode.description: The exit code from the tool execution.

executionTime.description: The execution time of the tool operation in milliseconds.

isError.description: Indicates whether the result represents an error. If true, the content contains error messages or exception information. If false, the content contains successful execution results.

accessDeniedException.description: The exception that occurs when you do not have sufficient permissions to perform an action. Verify that your IAM policy includes the necessary permissions for the operation you are trying to perform.

conflictException.description: The exception that occurs when the request conflicts with the current state of the resource. This can happen when trying to modify a resource that is currently being modified by another request, or when trying to create a resource that already exists.

internalServerException.description: The exception that occurs when the service encounters an unexpected internal error. This is a temporary condition that will resolve itself with retries. We recommend implementing exponential backoff retry logic in your application.

resourceNotFoundException.description: The exception that occurs when the specified resource does not exist. This can happen when using an invalid identifier or when trying to access a resource that has been deleted.

serviceQuotaExceededException.description: The exception that occurs when the request would cause a service quota to be exceeded. Review your service quotas and either reduce your request rate or request a quota increase.

throttlingException.description: The exception that occurs when the request was denied due to request throttling. This happens when you exceed the allowed request rate for an operation. Reduce the frequency of requests or implement exponential backoff retry logic in your application.

validationException.description: The exception that occurs when the input fails to satisfy the constraints specified by the service. Check the error message for details about which input parameter is invalid and correct your request.

nextToken.description: The token to use in a subsequent request to get the next set of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

items.description: The list of browser sessions that match the specified criteria.

nextToken.description: The token to use in a subsequent ListBrowserSessions request to get the next set of results.

browserIdentifier.description: The unique identifier of the browser associated with the session. This identifier specifies which browser environment is used for the session.

sessionId.description: The unique identifier of the browser session. This identifier is used in operations that interact with the session.

name.description: The name of the browser session. This name helps identify and manage the session.

status.description: The current status of the browser session. Possible values include ACTIVE, STOPPING, and STOPPED.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the browser session was created. This value is in ISO 8601 format.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The timestamp when the browser session was last updated. This value is in ISO 8601 format.

items.description: The list of code interpreter sessions that match the specified criteria.

nextToken.description: The token to use in a subsequent ListCodeInterpreterSessions request to get the next set of results.

codeInterpreterIdentifier.description: The unique identifier of the code interpreter associated with the session. This identifier specifies which code interpreter environment is used for the session.

sessionId.description: The unique identifier of the code interpreter session. This identifier is used in operations that interact with the session.

name.description: The name of the code interpreter session. This name helps identify and manage the session.

status.description: The current status of the code interpreter session. Possible values include ACTIVE, STOPPING, and STOPPED.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the code interpreter session was created. This value is in ISO 8601 format.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The timestamp when the code interpreter session was last updated. This value is in ISO 8601 format.

events.description: The list of events that match the specified criteria.

nextToken.description: The token to use in a subsequent request to get the next set of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

memoryId.description: The identifier of the AgentCore Memory resource containing the event.

actorId.description: The identifier of the actor associated with the event.

sessionId.description: The identifier of the session containing the event.

rootEventId.description: The identifier of the root event for this branch.

jobs.description: List of extraction job metadata matching the specified criteria.

nextToken.description: Token to retrieve the next page of results, if available.

jobID.description: The unique identifier for the extraction job.

messages.description: The messages associated with the extraction job.

messagesList.description: The list of messages that compose this extraction job.

status.description: The current status of the extraction job.

failureReason.description: The cause of failure, if the job did not complete successfully.

strategyId.description: The identifier of the memory strategy for this extraction job.

sessionId.description: The identifier of the session for this extraction job.

actorId.description: The identifier of the actor for this extraction job.

memoryRecordSummaries.description: The list of memory record summaries that match the specified criteria.

nextToken.description: The token to use in a subsequent request to get the next set of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

memoryRecordId.description: The unique identifier of the memory record.

text.description: The text content of the memory record.

memoryStrategyId.description: The identifier of the memory strategy associated with this record.

namespaces.description: The namespaces associated with this memory record.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the memory record was created.

score.description: The relevance score of the memory record when returned as part of a search result. Higher values indicate greater relevance to the search query.

sessionSummaries.description: The list of session summaries that match the specified criteria.

nextToken.description: The token to use in a subsequent request to get the next set of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

actorId.description: The identifier of the actor associated with the session.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the session was created.

memoryRecordSummaries.description: The list of memory record summaries that match the search criteria, ordered by relevance.

nextToken.description: The token to use in a subsequent request to get the next set of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

memoryRecordId.description: The unique identifier of the memory record.

text.description: The text content of the memory record.

memoryStrategyId.description: The identifier of the memory strategy associated with this record.

namespaces.description: The namespaces associated with this memory record.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the memory record was created.

score.description: The relevance score of the memory record when returned as part of a search result. Higher values indicate greater relevance to the search query.

sessionId.description: The unique identifier of the created browser session.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the browser session was created.

streams.description: The streams associated with this browser session. These include the automation stream and live view stream.

automationStream.description: The stream that enables programmatic control of the browser. This stream allows agents to perform actions such as navigating to URLs, clicking elements, and filling forms.

streamEndpoint.description: The endpoint URL for the automation stream. This URL is used to establish a WebSocket connection to the stream for sending commands and receiving responses.

streamStatus.description: The current status of the automation stream. This indicates whether the stream is available for use. Possible values include ACTIVE, CONNECTING, and DISCONNECTED.

liveViewStream.description: The stream that provides a visual representation of the browser content. This stream allows agents to observe the current state of the browser, including rendered web pages and visual elements.

streamEndpoint.description: The endpoint URL for the live view stream. This URL is used to establish a connection to receive visual updates from the browser session.

sessionId.description: The unique identifier of the created code interpreter session.

createdAt.description: The time at which the code interpreter session was created.

jobId.description: Extraction Job ID that was attempted to start.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The time at which the browser session was last updated.

sessionId.description: The identifier of the code interpreter session.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The timestamp when the code interpreter session was last updated.

runtimeSessionId.description: The ID of the session that you requested to stop.

statusCode.description: The status code of the request to stop the session.

streams.description: The collection of streams associated with a browser session in Amazon Bedrock. These streams provide different ways to interact with and observe the browser session, including programmatic control and visual representation of the browser content.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the browser stream was updated.

automationStream.description: The stream that enables programmatic control of the browser. This stream allows agents to perform actions such as navigating to URLs, clicking elements, and filling forms.

streamEndpoint.description: The endpoint URL for the automation stream. This URL is used to establish a WebSocket connection to the stream for sending commands and receiving responses.

streamStatus.description: The current status of the automation stream. This indicates whether the stream is available for use. Possible values include ACTIVE, CONNECTING, and DISCONNECTED.

liveViewStream.description: The stream that provides a visual representation of the browser content. This stream allows agents to observe the current state of the browser, including rendered web pages and visual elements.

streamEndpoint.description: The endpoint URL for the live view stream. This URL is used to establish a connection to receive visual updates from the browser session.
